embedded.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices with more than 50 MBs of memory and a more powerful processor. This Profile builds upon the Micro Embedded Device Server Profile. Besides various enhancements, this Profile requires that Servers expose OPC-UA types in the AddressSpace.
This Profile supersedes the Embedded 2022 UA Server Profile.
embedded.name: Embedded 2025 UA Server Profile

micro.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for small devices with limited resources. This Profile builds upon the Nano Embedded Device Server Profile. The most important additions is the support for subscriptions . A complete Type System is not required; however, if the Server implements any non-UA types then these types and their super-types shall be exposed.
This Profile supersedes the Micro Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile.
micro.name: Micro Embedded Device 2025 Server Profile

nano.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for chip level devices with limited resources. This Profile is functionally equivalent to the Core Server Facet and defines the "UA-TCP UA-SC UA-Binary" as the required transport profile including optional ECC Security Policy. The support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is optional for this Profile despite it being defined as “mandatory” in UA Part 5. Support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles. Exposing types in the AddressSpace is optional for this Profile except if custom types (i.e., types that are derived from well-known ObjectTypes, VariableTypes, ReferenceType or DataTypes) are used. Exposing all supported types in the AddressSpace is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles.
This Profile supersedes the Nano Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile.
nano.name: Nano Embedded Device 2025 Server Profile

standard.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices like a PC.
This Profile supersedes the Standard 2022 UA Server Profile.
standard.name: Standard 2025 UA Server Profile

conformance_units.description:
The non-twisted 384 or 512 bit curve described in http://www.faqs.org/rfcs/rfc5639.html. 
ECC public key compression is not used.
ECC coordinates are encoded as big endian integers padded with zeros. 
Signatures and keys are 96 and 128 bytes. 
An end entity Certificate shall use P384r1.
A CA that issues end entity Certificates shall use P384r1.
Other CAs may use P512r1.

conformance_units.description:
A keyed hash which is defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2104.
The hash algorithm is SHA1 and is described in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3174.
The URI is http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#hmac-sha1.
No known exploits exist when using SHA1 with a keyed hash, however, SHA1 was broken in 2017 so use of this algorithm is not recommended.
conformance_units.name: SymmetricSignatureAlgorithm_HMAC-SHA1

conformance_units.description:
The RSA signature algorithm which is defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3447.
The RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 scheme is used.
The hash algorithm is SHA1 and is described in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3174.
The URI is http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#rsa-sha1.
SHA1 was broken in 2017 so this algorithm should not be used.
conformance_units.name: AsymmetricSignatureAlgorithm_RSA-PKCS15-SHA1

conformance_units.description:
The RSA signature algorithm which is defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3447.
The RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 scheme is used. 
The hash algorithm is SHA2 with 256bits and is described in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6234. 
The URI is http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmldsig-more#rsa-sha256.
conformance_units.name: AsymmetricSignatureAlgorithm_RSA-PKCS15-SHA2-256

conformance_units.description:
The RSA encryption algorithm which is defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3447.
The RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 scheme is used.
The URI is http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-1_5.
The RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 scheme has known weaknesses and is not recommended.
conformance_units.name: AsymmetricEncryptionAlgorithm_RSA-PKCS15

conformance_units.description:
The RSA encryption algorithm which is defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3447.
The RSAES-OAEP scheme is used. 
The hash algorithm is SHA1 and is described in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6234.
The mask generation algorithm also uses SHA1.
The URI is http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#rsa-oaep.
No known exploits exist when using SHA1 with RSAES-OAEP, however, SHA1 was broken in 2017 so use of this algorithm is not recommended.
conformance_units.name: AsymmetricEncryptionAlgorithm_RSA-OAEP-SHA1

Servers shall allow administrators to disable/enable this token.
conformance_units.name: Security User Anonymous Server

conformance_units.description:
The UA Client supports the Anonymous User Identity Token.
Clients should allow administrators to disable/enable this token.
conformance_units.name: Security User Anonymous Client

conformance_units.description: Support managing users using the proper Methods on the "UserManagement" Object.
conformance_units.name: Security Role Client User Management

conformance_units.description: Uses and understands enumeration and structured DataTypes where the meta data and encoding information are exposed via the DataTypeDefinition Attribute.
conformance_units.name: Address Space Client DataTypeDefinition Attribute

conformance_units.description:
Support the DataTypeDefinition Attribute to expose the meta data and encoding information 
- for all Structure and Union DataTypes with a StructureDefinition DataType and
- for all Enumeration and OptionSet DataTypes with an EnumDefinition DataType..
Exposes the needed types DataTypeDefinition, StructureDefinition, StructureField, StructureType and EnumDefinition and their encoding Objects and all their supertypes in the AddressSpace.
conformance_units.name: Address Space DataTypeDefinition Attribute

conformance_units.description: The Server is configurable to expose at least one instance of the IetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType, or an Object instance that uses the IIetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType interface in the NetworkInterfaces folder.  All instances of IetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType, and Object instances that use the IIetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType interface shall be referenced by the NetworkInterfaces folder.
conformance_units.name: BNM Network Interface Instances

conformance_units.description: The Server is configurable to expose at least one instance of the IetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType, or an Object instance that uses the IIetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType interface in the NetworkInterfaces folder.  The Server is configurable that at least one of such instances also implements IVlanIdType.  All instances of IetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType, and Object instances that use the IIetfBaseNetworkInterfaceType interface shall be referenced by the NetworkInterfaces folder.
conformance_units.name: BNM Network Interface VLAN Instances

conformance_units.description: The Server is configurable to expose at least one instance of the PriorityMappingTableType in the MappingTables folder.  All instances of PriorityMappingTableType shall be referenced by the MappingTables folder.
conformance_units.name: BNM Mapping Instances

conformance_units.description: The Server is configurable to expose at least one instance of PriorityMappingTableType that provides the AddPriorityMappingEntry and DeletePriorityMappingEntry Methods.
conformance_units.name: BNM Priority Mapping Methods

conformance_units.description:
Implements User Access Level Security for at least one Variable Node for at least two different users. This includes an indication of read, write, historical read and historical write access to the Value Attribute.
The Variable Nodes shall be components of Object Instances.
conformance_units.name: Address Space User Access Level Base

conformance_units.description: Support AuditEvents for all Services supported by the Server.
conformance_units.name: Auditing Services

conformance_units.description: The Server supports the BaseModelChangeEvent and the NodeVersion Property for all Nodes that the Server allows model changes for.
conformance_units.name: Base Info Model Change

conformance_units.description: Exposes the AuditUpdateStateEventType and all its supertypes in the AddressSpace and one state machine providing Events of that Type.
conformance_units.name: Auditing UpdateStates

conformance_units.description: The Server returns available diagnostic information as requested with the 'returnDiagnostics' parameter.
conformance_units.name: Base Services Diagnostics

conformance_units.description:
Implement basic Service behaviour. This includes in particular:
 – checking the authentication token
 – returning the requestHandle in responses
 – respecting a timeoutHint
conformance_units.name: Session General Service Behaviour

conformance_units.description:
Implement basic Service behaviour. This includes in particular:
 – including the proper authentication token of the Session
 – creating a requestHandle if needed
 – evaluate the serviceResult and operational results
conformance_units.name: Session Client General Service Behaviour

conformance_units.description: Request diagnostic information with the 'returnDiagnostics' parameter.

conformance_units.description:
Support the MQTT version 5.0 transport protocol mapping defined in UA Part 14.

UA Applications shall switch to MQTT version v3.1.1 if the MQTT Server returns "Unsupported Protocol Version" in a connect request.
conformance_units.name: Protocol PubSub MQTT V5

conformance_units.description:
The Client as a minimum shall provide one or more of the following Facets to
* Lookup the AddressSpace (requires Browse Service)
* Reading Attributes
* Writing Attributes
* Use Subscriptions to monitor Attribute Values
* Use Subscriptions to monitor Events
* Invoke Methods

conformance_units.description: Defines the support of the SessionlessInvoke Service to process any of the Services (like Read/Write, Browse, or Call) that are designated for Session-less invocation.
conformance_units.name: Session Sessionless Invocation

conformance_units.description: Defines the use of the SessionlessInvoke Service to request one of the Services (like Read or Browse) that are allowed for sessionless invocation. UA Part 6 specifies which transports may be used and how.
conformance_units.name: Session Client SessionLess Service Calls

conformance_units.description: Exposes the VariableType ReferenceDescriptionVariableType, the ReferenceType HasReferenceDescription, the DataTypes ReferenceDescriptionDataType and ReferenceListEntryDataType and all their supertypes in the AddressSpace.

If signing is supported, the layout variant for integrity shall be supported also. If encryption is supported, the layout variant for confidentiality and integrity shall be supported also.

conformance_units.description: Support the Suppress2 and UnSuppress2 Methods to allow an operator control over the SuppressedState (including comments).

conformance_units.description:
Exposes the ObjectType ScheduleType, the DataTypes SpecialEventType, SpecialEventPeriodType, CalendarEntryType, DateType, Month, DayOfMonth, DayOfWeek, DateRangeType, TimeActionsType, BaseActionType, WriteLocalVariableActionType, CallLocalMethodActionType, TimeType and DailyScheduleType and all their supertypes in the AddressSpace.
Supports a configuration with at least two instances of ScheduleType having at least 12 entries of DaySchedule in their WeeklySchedule (overall, not per day), and 6 entries in the ExceptionSchedule each having at least 6 entries of ListOfTimeActions.
conformance_units.name: Scheduler Scheduling Base

conformance_units.description:
Exposes the ObjectType CalendarType, the DataTypes CalendarEntryType, DateType, Month, DayOfMonth, DayOfWeek, and DateRangeType and all their supertypes in the AddressSpace.
Supports a configuration with at least three instances of CalendarType, each having at least 10 entries in DateList.

The security keys may be fetched from a remote Key Server (SKS) using the GetSecurityKeys Method, pushed from an SKS using the SetSecurityKeys Method, or if the Publisher itself is a KeyServer using vendor-specific means.

An AuthenticatedEncryption algorithm which creates a Signature as part of the encryption process defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8439.
When using SignOnly, the encrypted data portion has zero length but it otherwise the same as SignAndEncrypt.
The key is generated using the algorithm specified in 2.6 of RFC8439.
conformance_units.name: SymmetricSignatureAlgorithm_ChaCha20Poly1305

conformance_units.description:
The UA Application supports the following:
1.	Query the DNS Server to resolve external IP addresses from Host names using a DNS resolver as described in RFC 1034.
conformance_units.name: Host Resolution – DNS Server

conformance_units.description:
The UA Application supports the following:
1.	Dynamically update the user-configured host name and IP with the DNS Server per RFC 2136.
conformance_units.name: Host Resolution – DNS Registration

conformance_units.description:
The UA Application supports the following as described in Host Table RFC 952:
1.	Read the user-configured local “Hosts” file to resolve external IP addresses from Host names.
conformance_units.name: Host Resolution – Local Host File

conformance_units.description:
The UA Application supports the following as described in the mDNS RFC 6762:
1.	Announce the user-configured host name and IP address and respond to host name queries using the mDNS protocol.
2.	Use mDNS queries to resolve external IP addresses from Host names.
conformance_units.name: Host Resolution – mDNS

conformance_units.description:
Support at least one of the optional Conformance Units that represent User Identity Token support  (User Name/Password, User X.509, User JWT IssuedToken, ...).
In case the Server support different kinds of User Identity tokens, the administrator shall be able to enable or disable individual tokens.
conformance_units.name: Security User Identity Token Support

This ConformanceUnit does not require queuing when multiple value changes occur during a "publish period".
I.e. the latest change will be sent in the Notification.

Includes exposing the EventTypes AuditEventType, AuditSecurityEventType, AuditChannelEventType	, AuditOpenSecureChannelEventType, AuditSessionEventType, AuditCreateSessionEventType, AuditActivateSessionEventType, AuditCancelEventType, AuditCertificateEventTypem AuditCertificateDataMismatchEventType, AuditCertificateExpiredEventType, AuditCertificateInvalidEventType, AuditCertificateUntrustedEventType, AuditCertificateRevokedEventType, and AuditCertificateMismatchEventType and all their supertypes in the AddressSpace.

conformance_units.description:
Support at least 500 MonitoredItems per Subscription.
This number has to be supported for at least half of the required Subscriptions for half of the required Sessions.

conformance_units.description: Support at least 10 MonitoredItems per Subscription where the size of each MonitoredItem is at least equal to size of Double.
conformance_units.name: Monitor Items 10

conformance_units.description:
Support at least 5 Publish Service requests per Session.
This number has to be supported for at least half of the minimum required sessions. Support, as a minimum, the number of Publish requests per session as the size of the NotificationMessage retransmission queue for Republish.
conformance_units.name: Subscription Publish Min 05

conformance_units.description: Support at least 20 monitored items with a queue size of 10000 for each item and where the size of each MonitoredItem is at least equal to size of Double. This requires storage capacity for 200 thousand values of DataType Double.
conformance_units.name: Subscription Durable StorageLevel Small

conformance_units.description: Support at least 100 monitored items with a queue size of 50000 for each item and where the size of each MonitoredItem is at least equal to size of Double. This requires storage capacity for 5 million values of DataType Double.
conformance_units.name: Subscription Durable StorageLevel Medium

conformance_units.description: Support at least 2000 monitored items with a queue size of 200000 for each item and where the size of each MonitoredItem is at least equal to size of Double. This requires storage capacity for 400 million values of DataType Double.
conformance_units.name: Subscription Durable StorageLevel High

conformance_units.description:
Support at least 5 Subscriptions per Session.
This number has to be supported for at least half of the minimum required sessions.

conformance_units.description:
Support at least 10 Publish Service requests per Session.
This number has to be supported for at least half of the minimum required sessions. Support, as a minimum, the number of Publish requests per session as the size of the NotificationMessage retransmission queue for Republish.

conformance_units.description:

Servers often will adapt the queue size to the number of currently MonitoredItems. However, it is expected that Servers support this minimum queue size for at least one third of the supported MonitoredItems.

conformance_units.description:
For each Object provided in the AddressSpace of the Server the ObjectType of the Object shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each Variable provided in the AddressSpace of the Server the VariableType and the DataType of the Variable shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each Value provided by the Server, which can be a subtype of the one defined in the Variable, the DataType of the Value shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each Method Argument provided by the Server the DataType of the Value shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each Structure DataType provided by the Server, each DataType used in the Structure shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server. 
For each Reference provided in the AddressSpace of the Server the ReferenceType shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each ObjectType, VariableType, ReferenceType, and DataType provided in the AddressSpace of the Server the direct supertype shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server. This requires that all supertypes shall be provided in the AddressSpace.
For each DataType having encoding information (Structured DataTypes) the Encoding Objects shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For each VariableType and ObjectType provided in the AddressSpace of the Server, all InstanceDeclarations shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server, even if they are optional and not used in any instance of the Server.
For each VariableType, ObjectType and DataType all Methods, Objects and Variables that are referenced with a hierarchical Reference either from the VariableType, ObjectType or DataType or an InstanceDeclaration of the ObjectType or VariableType, either directly or indirectly, shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
For all Events provided by the Server, the EventType shall be provided in the AddressSpace of the Server.
conformance_units.name: Base Info Type Information

conformance_units.description: The connection is established using TLS 1.1.  The application shall be configured to prevent TLS 1.0 connections, unless the TLS 1.0 connection is using TLS_RSA_WITH_RC4_128_SHA as described in ConformanceUnit "Security TLS_RSA_WITH_RC4_128_SHA"
conformance_units.name: Security TLS 1.1

conformance_units.description:
The Server supports a JSON Web Token (JWT) for user identity. UA Part 6 describes OAuth2 and JWTs in more detail. The use of this feature shall be able to be enabled or disabled by an Administrator.
The token will be encrypted if required by the security policy of the User Token Policy or by the security policy of the endpoint.
conformance_units.name: Security User JWT IssuedToken 2

conformance_units.description:
The Server supports a Kerberos Server token for User Identity. The use of this feature shall be able to be enabled or disabled by an Administrator. The use of this token is defined in Kerberos Token Documentation. 
The token will be encrypted if required by the security policy of the User Token Policy or by the security policy of the endpoint. An unencrypted token either requires message encryption or means outside the scope of OPC UA to secure the identity token so that it cannot be retrieved by sniffing the communication. One option would be a secure transport like a VPN.
conformance_units.name: Security User IssuedToken Kerberos

facets.description:
This Facet defines the core functionality required for any UA Server implementation. The core functionality includes the ability to discover endpoints, establish secure communication channels, create Sessions, browse the AddressSpace and read and/or write to Attributes of Nodes. The key requirements are: support for minimum one Session, support for the core AddressSpace structure, and the Server Capabilities Object, and authentication with UserName and Password. For broad applicability, it is recommended that Servers support multiple transport and security Profiles.
It supersedes the Core 2017 Server Facet.

profiles.description: This Facet defines the Server actions that are required for support of redundant Clients. Support of this Facet requires the implementation of the TransferSubscriptions Service which allows the transfer of Subscriptions from one Client’s Session to another Client’s Session.
profiles.name: Client Redundancy Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires the support of a hierarchy of Object Nodes that are notifiers and Nodes that are event sources. The hierarchy is commonly used as a way to organize a plant into areas that can be managed by different operators.
profiles.name: Address Space Notifier Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines a list of user documentation that a server application should provide.
profiles.name: Documentation Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for AlarmMetrics. AlarmMetrics expose status and potential issues in the alarm system. A Server can provide these metrics at various levels (operator station, plant area, overall system etc.).
profiles.name: A & C AlarmMetrics Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the capability of a Server (like a GDS) to provide access tokes to OPC UA Clients via an Authorization Service as defined in UA Part 12.
profiles.display_name: Global Service Authorization Request Server Facet
profiles.name: Global Service Authorization Request Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires providing the Information Model for Pull Management as defined in UA Part 12. For example KeyCredentials are needed to access an Authorization Service or a Broker. OPC UA Clients use this Information Model to request and update KeyCredentials they need.
profiles.name: Global Service KeyCredential Pull Facet

profiles.description:
This Facet defines the ability to expose meta data in the AddressSpace, i.e. data that describe the various OPC UA Types used by the Server.
Note that each Node defined in the core specification parts has ConformanceUnits defined that require the Node to be in the AddressSpace. If a Server supports such a ConformanceUnit, it shall expose the Nodes related to the ConformanceUnit in its AddressSpace.
profiles.name: Exposes Type System Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the standard support of subscribing to data changes and extends features defined by the Embedded Data Change Subscription 2021 Facet. Note that the Method Call Service is only required for the Methods defined in this Facet. This Facet supersedes the Standard DataChange Subscription 2017 Server Facet.
profiles.name: Standard DataChange Subscription 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires the support of Auditing which includes the Standard Event Subscription Server Facet. Support of this Facet requires that Audit Events be produced when a Client performs some action to change the state of the Server, such as changing the AddressSpace, inserting or updating a value etc. The auditEntryId passed by the Client is a field contained in every Audit Event and allows actions to be traced across multiple systems. The Audit Event Types and their fields shall be exposed in the Server’s AddressSpace. This Facet supersedes the Auditing Server Facet.
profiles.name: Auditing 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires the support of the Services that allow the Client to add, modify and delete Nodes in the AddressSpace. These Services provide an interface which can be used to configure Servers. This means all changes to the AddressSpace are expected to persist even after the Client has disconnected from the Server. This Facet supersedes the Node Management Server Facet.
profiles.name: Node Management 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application that acts as a global discovery server.
This Profile supersedes the Global Discovery Server 2017 Profile.
profiles.name: Global Discovery Server 2022 Profile

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application. that acts as a global discovery server and a global certificate manager.
This Profile supersedes the Global Discovery and Certificate Management 2017 Server.
profiles.display_name: Global Discovery and Certificate Mgmt 2022 Server
profiles.name: Global Discovery and Certificate Mgmt 2022 Server

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the requirements for a UA Server that wraps an OPC Alarm & Event (AE) Server (COM). This Profile identifies the sub-set of the UA Alarm & Condition model which is provided by the COM OPC AE specification. It is intended to provide guidance to developers who are creating servers that front end existing applications. It is important to note that some OPC A&E COM Servers may not support all of the functionality provided by an OPC UA A&C server, in these cases similar functionality maybe available via some non-OPC interface. For example if an A&E COM server does not support sending Alarm Acknowledgement messages to the system that it is obtaining alarm information from, this functionality may be available via some out of scope features in the underlying Alarm system. Another possibility is that the underlying system does not require acknowledgements or automatically acknowledges the alarm. It supersedes the A & E Wrapper Facet.
profiles.name: A & E Wrapper 2022 Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support of exposing File information via the defined FileType. This includes reading of file as well as optionally writing of file data.
profiles.name: File Access Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support of the OPC UA Information Model to expose configured user roles and permissions. It supersedes the User Role Base Server Facet.
profiles.name: User Role Base 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for Alarms with multiple sub-states that identify different limit Conditions. This facet builds upon the A&C Alarm Server Facet. The term exclusive means only one sub-state can be active at a time. For example, a temperature exceeds the HighHigh limit the associated exclusive LevelAlarm will be in the HighHigh sub-state and not in the High sub-state. This Facet requires that a Server support at least one of the optional Alarm models: Limit, RateOfChange or Deviation. This Facet supersedes the A & C Exclusive Alarming Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Exclusive Alarming 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Exclusive Alarming 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for Alarms with multiple sub-states that identify different limit Conditions. This Facet builds upon the A&C Alarm Server Facet. The term non-exclusive means more than one sub-state can be active at a time. For example, if a temperature exceeds the HighHigh limit the associated non-exclusive LevelAlarm will be in both the High and the HighHigh sub-state. This Facet requires that a server support at least one of the optional alarm models: Limit, RateOfChange or Deviation. This Facet supersedes the A & C Non-Exclusive Alarming Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Non-Exclusive Alarming 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Non-Exclusive Alarming 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for Alarms. Alarms extend the AcknowledgeableConditionType by adding an Active state which indicates when something in the system requires attention by an Operator. This Facet builds upon the A & C Acknowledgeable Alarm Server Facet. This facet requires that discrete AlarmTypes be supported, it also allows for optional support of shelving, alarm comments and other discrete AlarmTypes such as Trip or Off-Normal. This Facet supersedes the A & C Alarm Server Facet.
profiles.name: A & C Alarm 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description:
This Facet requires that Audit Events are generated for Alarms.
All optional Conformance Units are mandatory if the corresponding feature is supported; e.g., "A & C Latching Auditing" is mandatory if "A & C Latched State" in the "A & C Alarm Server Facet" is supported.
profiles.display_name: A & C Alarm Auditing Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Alarm Auditing Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support of Dialog Conditions. This Facet builds upon the A & C BaseCondition Server Facet Dialogs are ConditionTypes used to request user input. They are typically used when a Server has entered some state that requires intervention by a Client. For example, a Server monitoring a paper machine indicates that a roll of paper has been wound and is ready for inspection. The Server would activate a Dialog Condition indicating to the user that an inspection is required. Once the inspection has taken place the user responds by informing the Server of an accepted or unaccepted inspection allowing the process to continue. This Facet supersedes the A & C Dialog Server Facet.
profiles.name: A & C Dialog 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support for an Information Model used to provide industrial automation data. This model defines standard structures for analog and discrete data items and their quality of service. This Facet extends the Core Server Facet which includes support of the basic AddressSpace behaviour.
profiles.name: Data Access Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support of Dictionary References to relate Nodes in the AddressSpace to external dictionaries.
profiles.name: Dictionary Reference Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support of creating and accessing temporary files. This includes one of the defined access forms.
profiles.name: Temporary File Access Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires basic support for Conditions. Information about Conditions is provided through Event notifications and thus this Facet builds upon the Standard Event Subscription Server Facet. Conditions that are in an “interesting” state (as defined by the Server) can be refreshed using the Refresh Method. Optionally the server may also provide support for Condition classes. This Facet supersedes the A & C Base Condition Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Base Condition 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Base Condition 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support required for a Server to expose Alarms and Conditions in its AddressSpace. This includes the A & C AddressSpace information model. This Facet supersedes the A & C Address Space Instance Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Address Space Instance 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Address Space Instance 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires the enabling and disabling of Conditions. This Facet builds upon the A&C Base Condition Server Facet. Enabling and disabling also requires that instances of these ConditionTypes exist in the AddressSpace since the enable Method can only be invoked on an instance of the Condition. This Facet supersedes the A & C Enable Server Facet.
profiles.name: A & C Enable 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for Conditions with previous states that still require action on the part of the operator. This Facet builds upon the A&C Base Condition Server Facet. A common use case for this Facet is a safety critical system that requires that all Alarms be acknowledged even if it the original problem goes away and the Alarm returns to the inactive state. In these cases, the previous state with active Alarm is still reported by the Server until the Operator acknowledges it. When a Condition has previous states it will produce events with different Branch identifiers. When previous state no longer needs attention the branch will disappear. This Facet supersedes the A & C Previous Instances Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Previous Instances 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Previous Instances 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for Acknowledgement of active Conditions. This Facet builds upon the A&C Base Condition Server Facet. Acknowledgement requires support of the Acknowledge Method and the Acknowledged state. Support of the Confirmed state and the Confirm Method is optional. This Facet supersedes the A & C Acknowledgeable Alarm Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: A & C Acknowledgeable Alarm 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: A & C Acknowledgeable Alarm 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support of the CertificateExpirationAlarmType. It is used to inform Clients when the Server’s Certificate is within the defined expiration period. This Facet supersedes the A & C CertificateExpiration Server Facet.
profiles.name: A & C CertificateExpiration 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet enhances the A & C Base Condition Server Facet with support of the ConditionRefresh2 Method. This Facet supersedes the A & C Refresh2 Server Facet.
profiles.name: A & C Refresh2 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the basic functionality when supporting historical data access for raw data. This Facet supersedes the Historical Raw Data Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Raw Data 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support for the storage and retrieval of annotations for historical data. This Facet supersedes the Historical Annotation Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Annotation 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Data Update functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Data Update Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Data Update 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Data Insert functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Data Insert Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Data Insert 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Data Replace functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Data Replace Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Data Replace 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Data Delete functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Data Delete Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Data Delete 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the server requirements to support basic Historical Event functionality, including simple filtering and general access. This Facet supersedes the Base Historical Event Server Facet.
profiles.name: Base Historical Event 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Event update access functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Event Update Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Event Update 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Event insert access functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Event Insert Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Event Insert 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Event replace access functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Event Replace Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Event Replace 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet includes Historical Event delete access functionality. This Facet supersedes the Historical Event Delete Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Event Delete 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the handling of the aggregate filter when subscribing for Attribute values. This Facet supersedes the Aggregate Subscription Server Facet.
profiles.name: Aggregate Subscription 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the support for configuring the necessary information to validate access tokens when presented by a Client during session establishment. Access Tokens are issued by Authorization Services.
profiles.name: Authorization Service Server Facet

profiles.description: Defines the use of Sessionless Service invocation in a Server.
profiles.name: Sessionless Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the capability to interact with a Global Certificate Management Server to obtain an initial or renewed Certificate and Trust Lists.
profiles.name: Global Certificate Management Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support of reverse connectivity in a Server. Usually, a connection is opened by the Client before starting the UA-specific handshake. This will fail, however, when Servers are behind firewalls with no open ports to connect to. In the reverse connectivity scenario, the Server opens the connection and starts with a ReverseHello message requesting that the Client establish a Secure Channel using this connection.
profiles.name: Reverse Connect Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support of the RequestServerStateChange Method.
profiles.name: Request State Change Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support of StateMachines based on the types in UA Part "State Machines". This Facet supersedes the State Machine Server Facet.
profiles.name: State Machine 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support of Method invocation via the Call service. Methods are “lightweight” functions which are similar to the methods of a class found in any object-oriented programming language. A Method can have its scope bounded by an owning Object or an owning ObjectType. Methods with an ObjectType as their scope are similar to static methods in a class.
profiles.name: Method 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that the Server supports anonymous User Tokens.
profiles.name: User Token – Anonymous Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that a user token that is comprised of a username and password is supported. This user token can affect the behaviour of the ActivateSession Service.
profiles.display_name: User Token – User Name Password Server Facet
profiles.name: User Token – User Name Password Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that the use of an X509 certificates to identify users is supported.
profiles.display_name: User Token – X509 Certificate Server Facet
profiles.name: User Token – X509 Certificate Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support for JSON Web Tokens (JWT) to identify the user during Session setup. A JWT is the Access Token format which OPC UA requires when using OAuth2.
profiles.name: User Token – JWT Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that the historical Server supports reading data by specifying specific timestamps. This Facet supersedes the Historical Data AtTime Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Data AtTime 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that the server supports aggregate processing to produce derived values from raw historical data. This Facet supersedes the Historical Aggregate Server Facet.
profiles.name: Historical Aggregate 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support of reading modified historical values (values that where modified or inserted). This Facet supersedes the Historical Modified Data Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: Historical Access Modified Data 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: Historical Access Modified Data 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet indicates that the Server supports storage and retrieval of structured values for all supported access types. If a listed access type is supported then the corresponding optional ConformanceUnit shall be supported. This Facet supersedes the Historical Access Structured Data Server Facet.
profiles.display_name: Historical Access Structured Data 2022 Server Facet
profiles.name: Historical Access Structured Data 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines best practices for the configuration and management of Servers when they are deployed in a production environment. It provides the ability to enable or disable certain protocols and to configure the Discovery Server and specify where this Server shall be registered.
profiles.name: Base Server Behaviour Facet

profiles.description: This Facet describes the support of Variables with enumerated and or structured DataTypes. The Read, Write and Subscriptions service set shall support the encoding and decoding of these structured DataTypes. As an option the Server can also support alternate encodings, such as an XML encoding when the binary protocol is currently used and vice-versa.
profiles.name: ComplexType 2017 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support for ModelChangeEvents and the NodeVersion Property for Nodes. ModelChangeEvents are generated to indicate a change of the AddressSpace structure. The change may consist of adding or deleting a Node or Reference.
profiles.name: Model Change Event Server Facet

profiles.description: A Facet that describes the base characteristics for OPC UA Servers that support exposing schedules.
profiles.name: Scheduler Base Server Facet

profiles.description: A Facet that describes the base characteristics for OPC UA Servers that support configuring schedules.
profiles.name: Scheduler Configuration Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines the capability to update characteristics of individual Nodes in the AddressSpace by allowing writing to Node Attributes. It requires support for authenticating user access as well as providing information related to access rights in the AddressSpace and actually restricting the access rights as described.
profiles.name: Attribute WriteMask Server 2023 Facet

profiles.description:
This Facet defines the capability to update characteristics of individual Nodes in the AddressSpace by allowing writing to Node Attributes. It requires support for authenticating user access as well as providing information related to access rights in the AddressSpace and actually restricting the access rights as described.
 [Note: in the future this Facet will be Deprecated and replaced by "Attribute WriteMask Server 2023 Facet"]
profiles.name: Attribute WriteMask Server Facet

profiles.description: Support of this Facet enables discovery of the Server on a subnet using mDNS. This Facet is typically only implemented in Servers that do not register with an LDS.
profiles.name: Subnet Discovery Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet defines support of the OPC UA approach to manage user roles and permissions and to grant access to Nodes and Services based on the assigned roles and permissions. It supersedes the User Role Management Server Facet.
profiles.name: User Role Management 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the standard support for subscribing to events. Support of this Facet requires the implementation of Event Types representing the Events that the Server can report and their specific fields. This Facet supersedes the Standard Event Subscription Server Facet.
profiles.name: Standard Event Subscription 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies support of durable storage of data and events even when Clients are disconnected. This Facet implies support of any of the DataChange or Event Subscription Facets.
profiles.name: Durable Subscription 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description:
This Facet defines the capability of a Server to interact with a KeyCredential Service to obtain KeyCredentials. The Server provides the Information Model for Push Management specified as part of the KeyCredential Management in Part 12.
The Facet also includes the ProfileUris representing the authentication mechanism used to communicate with the resource that accepts KeyCredentials.

For example KeyCredentials are needed to access an Authorization Service or a Broker. The KeyCredential Service is typically part of a system-wide tool, like a GDS that also manages Applications, Access Tokens, and Certificates.
profiles.name: KeyCredential Service Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet requires support for transparent redundancy. If Servers implement transparent redundancy then the failover from one Server to another is transparent to the Client such that the Client is unaware that a failover has occurred; the Client is not required to do anything at all to keep data flowing. This type of redundancy is usually a hardware solution.
profiles.name: Redundancy Transparent Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the minimum level of support for data change notifications within subscriptions. It minimizes memory and processing overhead required to implement the Facet. This Facet includes functionality to create, modify and delete Subscriptions and to add, modify and remove Monitored Items. It is recommended that Servers shall support one Subscription with up to two items for each Session. In addition, support for two parallel Publish requests is suggested. This Facet is geared for a platform such as the Nano or Micro Embedded Device Server Profiles in which memory is limited and will be managed. It supersedes the Embedded DataChange Subscription Server Facet.
profiles.name: Embedded DataChange Subscription 2022 Server Facet

profiles.description: This Facet specifies the support for non-transparent redundancy. Failover for this type of redundancy requires the Client to monitor Server status and to switch to a backup Server if it detects a failure. The Server shall expose the methods of failover it supports (cold, warm or hot). The failover method tells the Client what it shall do when connecting to a Server and when a failure occurs. Cold redundancy requires a Client to reconnect to a backup Server after the initial Server has failed. Warm redundancy allows a Client to connect to multiple Servers, but only one Server will be providing values. In hot redundancy multiple Servers are able to provide data and a Client can connect to multiple Servers for the data.
profiles.name: Redundancy Visible Server Facet

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for chip level devices with limited resources. This Profile is functionally equivalent to the Core Server Facet and defines the "UA-TCP UA-SC UA-Binary" as the required transport profile including optional ECC Security Policy. The support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is optional for this Profile despite it being defined as “mandatory” in UA Part 5. Support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles. Exposing types in the AddressSpace is optional for this Profile except if custom types (i.e., types that are derived from well-known ObjectTypes, VariableTypes, ReferenceType or DataTypes) are used. Exposing all supported types in the AddressSpace is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles.
This Profile supersedes the Nano Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile.
profiles.name: Nano Embedded Device 2025 Server Profile

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for small devices with limited resources. This Profile builds upon the Nano Embedded Device Server Profile. The most important additions is the support for subscriptions . A complete Type System is not required; however, if the Server implements any non-UA types then these types and their super-types shall be exposed.
This Profile supersedes the Micro Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile.
profiles.name: Micro Embedded Device 2025 Server Profile

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices with more than 50 MBs of memory and a more powerful processor. This Profile builds upon the Micro Embedded Device Server Profile. Besides various enhancements, this Profile requires that Servers expose OPC-UA types in the AddressSpace.
This Profile supersedes the Embedded 2022 UA Server Profile.
profiles.name: Embedded 2025 UA Server Profile

profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices like a PC.
This Profile supersedes the Standard 2022 UA Server Profile.
profiles.name: Standard 2025 UA Server Profile

superseded_profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for chip level devices with limited resources. This Profile is functionally equivalent to the Core Server Facet and defines the "UA-TCP UA-SC UA-Binary" as the required transport profile including an ECC Security Policy. The support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is optional for this Profile despite it being defined as “mandatory” in UA Part 5. Support of Diagnostic Objects and Variables is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles. Exposing types in the AddressSpace is optional for this Profile except if custom types (i.e., types that are derived from well-known ObjectTypes, VariableTypes, ReferenceType or DataTypes) are used. Exposing all supported types in the AddressSpace is mandatory in some higher-level Profiles.
This Profile supersedes the Nano Embedded Device 2017 Server Profile.
superseded_profiles.name: Nano Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile

superseded_profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices with more than 50 MBs of memory and a more powerful processor. This Profile builds upon the Micro Embedded Device Server Profile. Besides various enhancements, this Profile requires that Servers expose OPC-UA types in the AddressSpace.
This Profile supersedes the Embedded 2017 UA Server Profile.
superseded_profiles.name: Embedded 2022 UA Server Profile

superseded_profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application for devices like a PC.
This Profile supersedes the Standard 2017 UA Server Profile.
superseded_profiles.name: Standard 2022 UA Server Profile

superseded_profiles.description:
Note: This Facet has been deprecated in v1.05, because it only adds limits and in 1.05 we require limits to be described in the product documentation.

This Facet specifies an enhanced support of subscribing to data changes. It is part of the Standard UA Server 2017 Profile. This Facet increases the limits defined by the Standard Data Change Subscription 2017 Server Facet.
superseded_profiles.name: Enhanced DataChange Subscription 2017 Server Facet

superseded_profiles.description:
This User Token has been deprecated with OPC UA Specification Version 1.05.
This Facet indicates that a User Token that is comprised of an issued token is supported.
superseded_profiles.display_name: User Token – Issued Token Server Facet
superseded_profiles.name: User Token – Issued Token Server Facet

superseded_profiles.description:
This User Token has been deprecated with OPC UA Specification Version 1.05.
This Facet further refines the User Token - Issued Token to indicate a windows implementation of Kerberos
superseded_profiles.display_name: User Token – Issued Token Windows Server Facet
superseded_profiles.name: User Token – Issued Token Windows Server Facet

superseded_profiles.description:
This Profile is a collection of Facets necessary to build a functional OPC UA Server Application specifically for small devices with limited resources. This Profile builds upon the Nano Embedded Device Server Profile. The most important additions is the support for subscriptions . A complete Type System is not required; however, if the Server implements any non-UA types then these types and their super-types shall be exposed.
This Profile supersedes the Micro Embedded Device 2017 Server Profile.
superseded_profiles.name: Micro Embedded Device 2022 Server Profile
